page: Vultr vc2-2c-4gb is a Cloud Compute virtual machine utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with the instance, consisting of an 80 GB NVMe SSD. No dedicated GPU hardware is included.

Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance across compute and memory subsystems. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 25% of tested hardware, with scores of 1226.09 and 2501.8 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show a sharp decline from 176.23 GB/sec for small cached blocks down to 19.26 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, reflecting the shared nature of the host resources. Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks both fall into the bottom 10%, with Redis achieving 1.36 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a budget-friendly tier designed for low-intensity workloads where resource density is prioritized over raw performance. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which can lead to variable performance under sustained loads. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, and small utility databases rather than high-throughput production workloads or intensive data processing.
description: Vultr vc2-2c-4gb is an entry-level Cloud Compute instance featuring 2 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks place this server in the lower tiers, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory write bandwidth both scoring in the bottom 25%. Database operations and Redis throughput also rank in the bottom 10%, reflecting the resource constraints of a shared CPU environment. This instance offers a highly cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require sustained high performance. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development and testing environments, and small-scale utility applications.
